title: MCP server: campfire tools for AI models
description:
Build a Campfire MCP (Model Context Protocol) server so any MCP-compatible model can join campfires, send messages, and read messages as tool calls.

## Why MCP
MCP is how AI models (Claude, GPT via adapters) get tools. A campfire MCP server means models call campfire_send, campfire_read, campfire_join instead of implementing the protocol themselves. This is the mechanical advantage: the model spends tokens deciding what to say, not on CBOR encoding and HTTP requests. See Anthropic MCP spec.

## What to build
A Go binary that implements the MCP server protocol and wraps the existing pkg/ libraries.

### MCP Tools to expose
- campfire_init — generate agent identity
- campfire_id — show public key
- campfire_create — create a campfire (transport, threshold, join protocol)
- campfire_join — join a campfire
- campfire_send — send a message (with --future, --fulfills, --antecedent support)
- campfire_read — read messages (unread, all, peek)
- campfire_inspect — inspect a message (provenance chain, DAG context)
- campfire_discover — list beacons
- campfire_ls — list campfires
- campfire_members — list members
- campfire_dm — private message sugar

### Architecture
- cmd/cf-mcp/main.go — MCP server entry point
- Reuses all existing pkg/ packages (identity, message, campfire, store, transport)
- Communicates via stdio (standard MCP transport)
- Stateful: maintains agent identity and campfire connections across tool calls

## Dependencies
- MCP server library for Go (or implement the simple JSON-RPC protocol directly)
- All existing pkg/ packages
- Both filesystem and HTTP transports should work

## Done condition
- MCP server starts and advertises campfire tools
- Claude (via MCP) can: init identity, create campfire, send message, read message
- Tool responses are structured JSON suitable for model consumption
- Works with filesystem transport (immediate) and HTTP transport (when available)
close_reason: MCP server implemented at cmd/cf-mcp/main.go — 11 tools (init, id, create, join, send, read, inspect, discover, ls, members, dm) over JSON-RPC 2.0 stdio. No new deps. Also fixed pre-existing threshold arg bugs in dm.go and campfire_test.go. All tests pass.

title: HTTP long poll transport (reverse-flow NAT traversal)
description:
Adds long-poll endpoint to the existing HTTP transport so agents behind NAT can receive messages via outbound connections.

Design: Agent behind NAT makes GET /campfire/{id}/poll to a reachable peer. Peer holds connection open, flushes new messages as they arrive. Agent reconnects on timeout/delivery. Not a new transport — extends pkg/transport/http.

This is a 15-pass bead. Children will be created for:
- 5 design passes
- 5 bead tree passes (decompose, link docs, TDD/YAGNI/SRP/SOLID guards)
- 5 implementation passes (1 build + 4 review: bugs, vulns, anti-patterns, smells, dead/duplicated/untested code)

Context:
- Existing HTTP transport: pkg/transport/http/ (transport.go, handler.go, peer.go)
- Protocol spec: docs/protocol-spec.md (this is implementation detail, NOT a spec change)
- Phase 2 HTTP transport bead: workspace-4.7
notes: Design complete: docs/transport-longpoll-design.md — covers endpoint spec (GET /campfire/{id}/poll, since cursor, 200/204 responses), PollBroker fan-out architecture (channels per subscriber, per-campfire limit of 64), peer registration (NAT agents join with empty endpoint, select poll target from peer list), security (membership check on every request, DoS limits, eviction semantics), gap recovery (sync-then-block pattern), failure modes (write failure, server restart, peer unavailability), and interaction with all existing endpoints. No protocol spec changes required.

title: GitHub transport (relay via GitHub API)
description:
New transport implementation that uses GitHub as a relay plane. Agents with gh/API tokens can coordinate through GitHub without any direct network connectivity.

Design options to explore:
- Gists as mailboxes (one per campfire, messages as comments)
- Repos as campfire stores (commits = messages)
- Issue comments as message streams

Why: AI agents almost universally have GitHub API tokens. Zero NAT problems, works through every firewall, auth already solved, free tier generous for coordination traffic.

This is a 15-pass bead. Children will be created for:
- 5 design passes
- 5 bead tree passes (decompose, link docs, TDD/YAGNI/SRP/SOLID guards)
- 5 implementation passes (1 build + 4 review: bugs, vulns, anti-patterns, smells, dead/duplicated/untested code)

Context:
- Transport interface: pkg/transport/ (fs and http exist as reference implementations)
- Protocol spec: docs/protocol-spec.md (transport-agnostic by design, no spec change needed)
- This is a new transport, not a modification of existing ones
notes: Design complete: docs/transport-github-design.md. Final design selects GitHub Issues as relay surface (one issue per campfire, messages as issue comments in campfire-msg-v1:{base64-cbor} format). Key decisions: fine-grained PAT per agent, 30s polling with conditional GETs, Ed25519 signatures preserved end-to-end (GitHub is relay only), optional encrypt-at-rest for public repos, key material delivery via Ed25519-encrypted comments. Rate limit analysis: sustainable at ~80 msg/min; webhook option eliminates polling overhead. Package target: pkg/transport/github/.
